title: Salesforce to acquire Fin for $3.6B to strengthen Agentforce
friendlyTitle: Salesforce is buying Fin to boost AI customer support
summary: Salesforce signed an agreement to buy Fin, the AI customer-service platform formerly known as Intercom, for about $3.6 billion. Salesforce says Fin's team and technology will deepen Agentforce's service-agent capabilities across chat, WhatsApp, SMS, phone, Slack, and other support channels.
friendlySummary: Salesforce plans to buy Fin, an AI customer-service platform, for about $3.6 billion. The goal is to make Salesforce’s Agentforce better at resolving customer questions across many channels.
whyItMatters: Customer service is one of the first enterprise workflows where AI agents can produce measurable returns. For businesses, the deal is a sign that support automation is becoming core CRM infrastructure rather than a bolt-on chatbot.
friendlyWhyItMatters: If you run or rely on customer support, expect more AI agents handling routine issues before a human steps in. The useful question is how well these systems escalate edge cases and keep context intact.
